activity_title: Replaced dashboard mock data with corrected live data
activity_summary: Regenerated the full dashboardData from live PostgreSQL in one consolidated query and spliced it into leakage_dashboard.html (data-block-only). Corrected methodology: template-inclusive shipping (carrier_charge + shipping_template_price) for L2/L3/L5, ASIN-grain L3 with PPC joined via ref_id, SKU-grain L4 with distinct-order refund rate, and ALL flagged rows embedded so the live KPI counts equal true totals. Counts L1 149 / L2 240 / L3 42 / L4 33 / L5 10.
evidence_refs: validator PASS; counts 149/240/42/33/10; prompt_output CSVs dated 2026-06-29
reusable_pattern: Single consolidated SQL to JSON to Python marker-splice; embed all flagged rows so countFor(rows) equals true totals
validation_rule: Dashboard data block generated from one query execution; counts derived from embedded rows
gap_or_risk: counts.l1 must equal distinct ASIN of the L1 detail (grain consistency)
next_action: Keep counts.l1 defined as distinct ASIN of the detail set

activity_summary: Fixed validator inconsistencies and execution blockers in refresh_dashboard.py: scoped the forbidden-table scan (check 5) to the data sections so verification_summary may document excluded tables; corrected the L1 guard (check 4) to compare distinct-ASIN rather than row length (L1 is ASIN+SKU grain); added Bash to allowedTools so the headless splice script can execute; raised WLSP_REFRESH_TIMEOUT default from 1500 to 5400 seconds.
evidence_refs: refresh.log RESULT PASS 2026-06-29 15:21; ALLOWED includes Bash; TIMEOUT_SEC=5400
reusable_pattern: Validator scans data sections only for forbidden tables; L1 validated by distinct-ASIN; headless writer needs Bash to splice large data blocks file-to-file
validation_rule: Forbidden-table scan excludes verification_summary; counts.l1 equals distinct ASIN in L1 detail
gap_or_risk: A 100KB-plus data block cannot be Edited inline by the LLM, so it needs a Bash splice script
next_action: Consider single-query to Python-splice to remove headless-write fragility

activity_title: Verified manual vs automated dashboard outputs
activity_summary: Ran refresh_dashboard.py headless and diffed its output against the manual snapshot. L1-L5 detail rows, account_summary and ph_summary are content-identical and counts equal. Remaining differences are metadata only: ph_status enrichment tags on UNATTRIBUTED rows and the displayed.l5 convention. Earlier runs surfaced a 1500s timeout, a Bash-permission block, and a counts.l1 148-vs-149 grain desync, all since resolved.
evidence_refs: diff: 0 row diffs and 0 value diffs across l1-l5; refresh.log
reusable_pattern: Multi-query assembly can desync counts vs arrays under live data; compute counts from the same query as the arrays
validation_rule: Manual and automated outputs must match on detail-row content; total count is time-dependent
gap_or_risk: counts.l1 grain desync when an ASIN spans two SKUs or converts on one SKU only
next_action: Pin counts.l1 to distinct-ASIN of the detail set in the prompt

activity_title: Resolved inflated-figures report (OCR plus window boundary)
activity_summary: Investigated a verification claiming the HTML figures were inflated and possibly mixing LEDSone data. Proved the HTML matches the DB to the penny for its window. The reported gaps were two artifacts: OCR misreads in the screenshot column (6 read as 8, 3 read as 5, garbled ASIN strings) and a one-day window boundary (the verification used Jun 23-28 while the query window included Jun 22). No LEDSone contamination (ledsone_spend=0) and no multi-week window.
evidence_refs: per-date breakdown; html_spend equals DB Jun22-28; gap equals the Jun 22 contribution for every flagged ASIN
reusable_pattern: When figures look inflated, decompose by date, account and record_type and confirm grain plus window before assuming contamination
validation_rule: HTML detail must equal the DB aggregate for the defined window; gaps attributable to boundary or live drift, not contamination
gap_or_risk: Ambiguous last-7-days boundary: date >= CURRENT_DATE-7 spans 8 calendar days
next_action: Pin the window to the previous Monday-Sunday week

activity_title: Weekly Monday-Sunday window plus data-driven Start/End dates
activity_summary: Implemented the Monday-run contract: fetch the previous complete week (Monday-Sunday) using date_trunc week CURRENT_DATE bounds for L1/L2/L3 (L4 = 30 days ending Sunday; L5 = last 3 complete months). Set report_date to the previous Sunday so the header derives Start = report_date minus 6 (Monday) and End = report_date (Sunday), displaying the true range 2026-06-22 to 2026-06-28. Added a Reporting-window section to the prompt.
evidence_refs: prev_monday 2026-06-22 / prev_sunday 2026-06-28; header Start 06-22 End 06-28; 14 bounded predicates
reusable_pattern: Anchor weekly windows to date_trunc week CURRENT_DATE (robust if run off-Monday); set report_date = week-end so the header shows Monday-Sunday; derive display dates from data not run date
validation_rule: Start/End must equal the fetched data Monday-Sunday window; never include the current partial week
gap_or_risk: Header derived Start/End from the run date (showed Jun 23-29) instead of the data (Jun 22-28)
next_action: Optionally store explicit data_start and data_end in summary

activity_title: Margin Trend (L5) made account-aware (split by account)
activity_summary: Fixed the reported bug where All Accounts, LEDSone UK and DCVoltage UK showed an identical Margin Trend. Root cause: L5 had no account dimension (Q_L5 grouped by ph and month; renderL5 never filtered by account). Rewrote Q_L5 with GROUPING SETS to emit per (PH, account, month) trends plus a combined account=ALL slice, with declining computed per series. Updated renderL5 and countFor to filter L5 by the selected account (ALL for All-accounts). Regenerated L5 to 213 rows. LEDSone (9 declining) and DCVoltage (8) now differ from ALL (10).
evidence_refs: L5 accounts ALL/LEDSone UK/DCVoltage UK; 213 rows; Jasmini May ALL -17.7 LED -34.0 DCV -7.5; ALL equals sum of accounts
reusable_pattern: For per-account plus combined metrics use GROUPING SETS to emit account rows plus an ALL rollup; renderer maps __ALL__ to ALL
validation_rule: Every L5 row carries account; ALL equals the sum of per-account; the account filter segments L5
gap_or_risk: Prior L5 ignored account, producing misleading identical views across accounts
next_action: Confirm with TL that per-account declining flags are the desired action signal

activity_title: L1 detail content verification vs live DB
activity_summary: Content-verified the L1 dataset against live PostgreSQL, ignoring the count which is time-dependent. All 149 records satisfy every rule (Amazon-only, UK, last 7 days, spend over 3, conversions=0, valid ASIN, correct account and PH). Zero invalid records; zero missing (invalid-ASIN exclusions legitimately 0; UK filter precise at 72,478 rows). Manual equals live content (0 diffs). Documented the 148-vs-149 grain distinction (ASIN-rollup count vs ASIN+SKU detail) caused by 3 boundary ASINs.
evidence_refs: 0 rule violations; account map amazon Dcvoltage and amazon Ledsone; boundary ASINs B094DN1XL1, B096FPKPNQ, B0CG5ZHJYF
reusable_pattern: Verify detail by re-deriving from source and auditing rule violations plus exclusions; treat count drift as expected
validation_rule: L1 detail is every asin+sku+ph+account with spend over 3 and orders=0 in Amazon/UK/last-week/ad; counts.l1 = distinct ASIN of that detail
gap_or_risk: ASIN-rollup count (148) differs from detail distinct ASIN (149): a grain artifact, not an error
